trivia_bullets.text: Feat of Resistance originated in Khans of Tarkir (September 2014, set no. KTK 10). Khans of Tarkir depicted the five warring clans of a plane where dragons had been driven extinct — the Abzan clan, associated with endurance and white-black-green colors, was the thematic home for this card. The original KTK flavor text reads: "Dragons are extinct on Tarkir, but Abzan magic still emulates their endurance." [Scryfall: KTK/10].

EDHREC rank 10113 (as of 2026-05-19). The card is Pauper-legal and PauperCommander-legal — one of relatively few protection instants in those formats. Its +1/+1 counter adds permanent power where most protection spells are purely defensive [Scryfall].

The M21 printing carries no flavor text — the KTK Abzan flavor text did not travel with the card into Core Set 2021. The art by David Gaillet is consistent across all three printings: Khans of Tarkir (2014), The List (2019), and Core Set 2021 (2020). No new art was commissioned for M21 [Scryfall prints search].

Protection from a chosen color is one of the most versatile protective keywords in MTG — it simultaneously prevents blocking, targeting, damage, and enchanting/equipping from that color, making this a flexible combat trick that can save a creature from targeted removal, force it through a blocker of the right color, or strip it of an opposing aura in a single instant-speed action [Scryfall oracle text].
